title: Potentilla zapateriana
intro: Potentilla zapateriana, also known as Zapatera cinquefoil, is a perennial herbaceous plant belonging to the Rosaceae family, native to the Iberian Peninsula. This plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antidiabetic properties, which may contribute to its use in traditional medicine. The bioactive constituents of Potentilla zapateriana include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and triterpenes, which are responsible for its medicinal activities. The plant is typically used to prepare herbal teas, infusions, and decoctions, which are used to treat various health conditions, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
taxonomy: Potentilla zapateriana belongs to the family Rosaceae and is a species of flowering plant in the genus Potentilla. It is native to the Iberian Peninsula. The classification of Potentilla zapateriana is as follows: kingdom Plantae, clade Angiosperms, clade Eudicots, clade Rosids, order Rosales, family Rosaceae, genus Potentilla, species P. zapateriana. This classification is based on its morphological and molecular characteristics. Potentilla zapateriana is a member of the large and diverse family Rosaceae, which includes many other species of flowering plants.

parts: Potentilla zapateriana, also known as Zapatera cinquefoil, is a species of cinquefoil.
